reasoning: Response hits Tajima Beef (maff_gi:2) as first result and immediately follows with Kobe Beef (maff_gi:3) — exactly the provenance link the query asked about. MAFF GI official data with high-confidence translation. Minor caveat: 195 items returned without filtering to the two relevant beef items; agent must extract signal from noise.
missing_data: No date of typical availability, no venue suggestions (where to taste Tajima beef), no price context

reasoning: Arabic query served with Arabic translations — constraint satisfied (RULE D does not penalise). Same authoritative MAFF GI data as L1-01, Arabic descriptions are accurate translations. Minor caveat: same 195-item dump issue; but the top two items are the correct Tajima/Kobe beef pair.
missing_data: No venue or tasting location suggestions in Arabic
improvement_hint: Same as L1-01: keyword routing to surface the named GI item first; transliteration of product names could be improved (ساو موتشي دايزو is phonetic, not translated)

reasoning: Query explicitly asked about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields — a very specific rural municipality in southern Kyoto. Tool returned get_japan_heritage for Kyoto prefecture, which surfaces Kyoto City heritage (Nijo Castle, To-ji, Nanzen-ji) — none related to Minami-Yamashiro village or tea cultivation. The tool_scope_note itself warns this tool is not for specific landmarks. Wrong tool for this intent; per RULE A data returned is real/authoritative so groundedness/accuracy hold, but intent match is 2.
missing_data: Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, the Kyoto Prefecture tea cultivation GI, any mention of the village's scenic tea terrace landscapes
improvement_hint: Route tea-field queries to get_local_food or get_local_specialty with municipality='南山城村'; alternatively search_area with q='南山城 茶' would surface relevant scrape data

reasoning: Spanish query about Yoshida no Hi-Matsuri correctly returned with the festival as first item (bunka_intangible:Q11413521) with Spanish translation 'Festival del fuego de Yoshida'. Correct prefecture (Yamanashi/Fujiyoshida). Response in Spanish satisfies language constraint. One caveat: date field is null — the festival is held Aug 26-27 but this is missing.
missing_data: Festival date (late August), description of the fire ritual mechanics, access from Fuji-Q Highland area
improvement_hint: Enrich bunka_intangible records with known festival dates from Wikidata P585/P580 where available; the Yoshida Hi-Matsuri date is well-documented

reasoning: Query asks specifically about 中芸地域 yuzu (Nakagei district yuzu — GI maff_gi:96 is actually 物部ゆず from Kami City, which is adjacent but not Nakagei). The response is truncated at 500/651 items without filtering; the specific 中芸ゆず GI (if it exists as a separate registration) may be buried or absent. 物部ゆず is the closest relevant item and appears first, but Nakagei yuzu (GI 中土佐/奈半利区域) is distinct. Also, several scraped items are noise (temple descriptions, tourist route articles). Per RULE C: authoritative data present but scope/filtering gap → 3.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず as a distinct GI or production-area entry; Kitagawa village and Umaji village yuzu production context
improvement_hint: Add municipality-level filtering to get_local_food so 'Nakagei' queries can be narrowed; the 651-item truncation with noise items hurts agent utility

reasoning: Chinese query about Hirosaki Park cherry blossoms. Response correctly returns Hirosaki Castle (Q328582, sakura_meisho_100, nihon_100_meijo) and Hirosaki Park (Q11288816, sakura_meisho_100) as top results — exactly what the user needs. Rich structured data with coordinates, hours, website. Language constraint: query is zh but response is in en/ja with name_zh fields populated — per RULE D, constraint_handling -1 from otherwise 4 = 3. Good practical data overall.
missing_data: Cherry blossom season dates (late April to early May), festival schedule during hanami season
improvement_hint: When lang param is missing from get_spots, infer from query language or document that agents should pass lang='zh'

reasoning: Korean query about Izumo Taisha. search_area returns Izumo Taisha (Q696362) as first result with National Treasure designation, correct coordinates, opening hours, website, free entry. reasoning: French query about Itsukushima Shrine but response is in English (lang='en' passed, not 'fr'). Per RULE D, constraint_handling = 2 (language mismatch is constraint failure; applying RULE B this is the single worst dim). Response data itself is excellent: Itsukushima Shrine at top (Q191763) with UNESCO WHS designation, National Treasure, correct coordinates, full structure hierarchy. Groundedness/accuracy unaffected per RULE B. Practical info is good in English but fails the French speaker's constraint.
missing_data: French-language descriptions, ferry access from Miyajimaguchi
improvement_hint: Agent called with lang='en' instead of lang='fr'; server should accept and serve 'fr' translations — verify fr translation coverage for top shrines

reasoning: German query about Kumano Kodo but tool called with lang='en'. Per RULE D, constraint_handling = 2. reasoning: Vietnamese query about Yumihama Kasuri (弓浜絣, a traditional kasuri cotton textile from Tottori). Tool returned get_local_specialty for Tottori which shows only 7 items — all MAFF GI foods (rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon). Per RULE A, wrong-tool-adjacent: the tool could have returned craft data but did not. Score 2 for intent since the specific craft is entirely absent.

reasoning: Query specifically asks for 宿坊 (shukubo — temple lodging) for the Shikoku Henro pilgrimage. Tool returned get_hotels for Tokushima which shows ryokan and guest houses (OSM-sourced) but none are identified as shukubo or henro-specific lodging. The Shikoku henro has ~88 temples each potentially offering shukubo — this data class is absent. Returned items are generic ryokan/hotels not tagged as pilgrimage lodging. Intent essentially missed: authoritative OSM/Wikidata hotel data is present but the specific shukubo class is entirely absent.

Tool returned get_spots for Hokkaido which gives tourist attractions (Showa-Shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle) — no farm experience/agri-tourism data at all. This is a complete retrieval failure for the intent. Per RULE A: data returned is real Wikidata spots but entirely off-intent. Correct tool would be search_semantic or search_hybrid with query about agricultural experiences, or get_local_specialty for food products with venue info.
missing_data: Farm stay (農家民宿) listings, agricultural experience programs, flower farm tour operators in Hokkaido (Furano/Biei lavender farms, dairy farm tours)

reasoning: English query about traditional pottery in Kyushu. Response returns Imari-Arita Ware and Karatsu Ware (both METI official designations) — exactly what a pottery-interested traveler needs, with production area text naming specific towns to visit. Also includes Hakata Ori (textile) which expands scope slightly. The see_also_wikidata_venues section adds Takeo City Library museum. One caveat: query asks 'where can I see it being made' — response gives craft designations but not specific kiln/workshop venues.

reasoning: Korean query about Tohoku traditional festivals. Tool called only for Aomori, returning Hachinohe Sansha Taisai and Aomori Nebuta — both excellent and with correct Korean translations. However Tohoku has 6 prefectures and many famous festivals: Akita Kanto, Sendai Tanabata, Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa Odori, Fukushima Waraji Matsuri. Single-prefecture coverage misses the regional scope. Per RULE C: authoritative data for one prefecture, regional scope gap → 3. national_heritage section adds UNESCO items but these are not Tohoku-specific.
